title: Define Project Context in a Project Initiation Document (PID)
summary: Identify the essential elements to include in the context section of a PID—business drivers, purpose, problem statement, high‑level goals, success criteria, and a concise mission statement—to ensure alignment with organisational strategy from the outset.
content:
The context section answers: why the client is pursuing the project, what problem it solves, and how it aligns with organisational strategy. Include:
- Business drivers (strategic, technical, market).
- Project purpose and problem statement.
- High‑level goals and objectives.
- Success definition and measurable metrics.
- A concise mission statement.
This information helps keep the team focused on delivering value and makes it easier to evaluate scope changes later.
can_answer: What details belong in the context section of a Project Initiation Document?
examples: Business driver: Reduce manual processing time by 30%.
Goal: Launch the e‑commerce platform by Q3 2024.
warnings: Avoid vague statements; ensure each driver links to measurable outcomes.
